data.narrative:
Hubble Network is a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Alex Haro and Ben Wild, currently valued at $400M post-money on $100M raised. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, including Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), and several Series C firms like Axiom Space ($2.5B) and Varda Space Industries ($1.5B). Five public comparables—RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL—provide sector context though cannot directly anchor valuation absent revenue disclosure.

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a standard 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to the $400M base, implies $760M. The peer-median method across 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields $1.4B, with the comp range spanning $400M to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $646M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion—reflecting the $400M to $2.5B range in the peer set—indicates material uncertainty around whether Hubble should trade toward early-stage or later-stage multiples within the Space & Aerospace sector. Resolution hinges on differentiating Hubble's technical maturity, addressable market, and unit economics relative to closest-stage peers like Cowboy and Aalyria.
